[[JA:高椅神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11670742}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2014年7月}} {{Otheruses||{{ill|lt=Takahashi Shrine (Mishima)|Q11670742|WD=Q11670742}} in {{ill|lt=Mishima|Q653478|WD=Q653478}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}}} {{神社 | 名称=Takahashi Shrine | 画像=[[File:高椅神社楼門.jpg|270px|Takahashi Shrine rōmon]]
Rōmon (Tochigi Prefecture designated cultural property) | 所在地={{ill|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|Q44843|WD=Q44843}}{{ill|lt=Oyama|Q374511|WD=Q374511}} Takahashi 702 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill||lt=Iwaka Mutsukari no Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}
{{ill||lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}
Amakagami-no-Mikoto
Amayorozu-no-Mikoto | 別名=Carp Myōjin, Takahashi Myōjin | 社格={{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}（small）・former {{ill||lt=prefectural shrine|UNKNOWN}} | 創建=(trad.) {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723}} 41 ({{ill|lt=111|Q30640|WD=Q30640}}) | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | 例祭=First Saturday of October | 神事=Dedication of daidai-kagura | 地図={{Location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Takahashi Shrine|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250}} }} '''Takahashi Shrine''' (たかはしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Oyama|Q374511|WD=Q374511}}, {{ill|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|Q44843|WD=Q44843}}. It is a {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was prefectural shrine. It is the oldest shrine whose main deity is {{ill||lt=Iwaka Mutsukari no Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}, revered as the "ancestral deity of {{ill|lt=cuisine|Q1778821|WD=Q1778821}}." Associated deities are Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto, Amakagami-no-Mikoto, Amayorozu-no-Mikoto, {{ill||lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Kami|UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286}}, {{ill||lt=Takaokami-no-Kami|UNKNOWN}}, {{ill||lt=Homusubi-no-Kami|UNKNOWN}}, and {{ill||lt=Toyouke-hime-no-Kami|UNKNOWN}}. It has four branch shrines in {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277}} and is worshiped by people in culinary professions. * Hida Takahashi Shrine ({{ill|lt=Gero Onsen Gassho Village|Q11360703|WD=Q11360703}}) * Keihoku Takahashi Shrine ({{ill|lt=Yoake no Mori|Q17209177|WD=Q17209177}} in Nakatsugawa) * Kane Takahashi Shrine (inside Kane Shrine in Gifu) * Hida Takayama Takahashi Shrine (inside Hie Shrine in Takayama) ===History=== According to shrine tradition, in {{ill|lt=111|Q30640|WD=Q30640}} (Keikō 41), when {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258}} campaigned eastward, he invited Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto, Amakagami-no-Mikoto, and Amayorozu-no-Mikoto here and prayed for victory. When Emperor Keikō later inspected the campaign sites, Iwaka Mutsukari no Mikoto, of the {{ill||lt=Kashiwade no Omi|UNKNOWN}} line, was old and with imperial permission remained here. In {{ill|lt=684|Q31128|WD=Q31128}} ({{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181}} 12), descendants of Iwaka Mutsukari who ruled this area were granted the surname Takahashi Ason, and in that year they merged their ancestral deity Iwaka Mutsukari at this shrine and called it Takahashi Shrine. In {{ill|lt=1029|Q19274|WD=Q19274}} ({{ill|lt=Chōgen|Q1193557|WD=Q1193557}} 2), when a well was dug in the precincts, a large {{ill|lt=carp|Q81110|WD=Q81110}} appeared. Reported to the capital, {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Ichijō|Q374087|WD=Q374087}} deemed it truly miraculous and granted the imperial plaque "Japan’s sole carp-forbidden shrine." Since then, parishioners do not eat carp, do not use vessels painted with carp, and do not raise {{ill|lt=koinobori|Q779745|WD=Q779745}} in May. From this comes the alternate name '''Carp Myōjin'''. In the medieval period it was protected by the {{ill|lt=Yūki clan|Q2975399|WD=Q2975399}} of {{ill|lt=Shimōsa Province|Q1053695|WD=Q1053695}}. More than 20 documents remain, including donation letters, prayers, and practice records from castle lords {{ill|lt=Yūki Masaasahi|Q11606587|WD=Q11606587}}, {{ill|lt=Yūki Masanao|Q11606589|WD=Q11606589}}, {{ill|lt=Yūki Masakatsu|Q8062739|WD=Q8062739}}, {{ill|lt=Yūki Harutomo|Q8062736|WD=Q8062736}}, and {{ill|lt=Yūki Hideyasu|Q1130929|WD=Q1130929}}. Even after Hideyasu was transferred to Echizen Fukui, ceremonial proxy visits were maintained generation after generation until the Meiji Restoration. ===Cultural properties=== === Tochigi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property === * Rōmon - Donated by the Mizuno family, lords of Yūki Castle; construction took 16 years from Hōreki 4 to Meiwa 7 ({{ill|lt=1770|Q7683|WD=Q7683}}). Restoration was carried out from 2017 to 2018. === Oyama City designated intangible cultural property === * Daidai Kagura - dedicated at the autumn annual festival. === Chinowa crossing === The chinowa ring at Takahashi Shrine has a rare square shape, about 2 m per side, not circular. The reason is unknown. It is made with one-sided reed as core, wrapped in makomo and bound with cogon-grass ropeYomiuri Shimbun Tochigi edition, August 3, 2017, p.29.. === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === * Kōten Kōkyūjo / National Priests’ Association corrected edition [{{NDLDC|1442211}} ''Engishiki, upper volume'' p.319] (Ōokayama Shoten, 1929) - Modern Digital Library ===External links=== *[https://www.facebook.com/TAKAHASHIJINJA Engishiki Takahashi Shrine] (official site) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかはししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takahashi Shrine (Q11670742)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takahashi Shrine (Q11670742)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Takahashi Shrine (Q11670742)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:栃木県の神社]] [[Category:下総国の式内社]] [[Category:栃木県の旧県社]] [[Category:小山市の歴史]] [[Category:小山市の建築物]] [[category:日本の食文化|神たかはししんしや]] [[Category:高橋氏|社たかはししんしや]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QTakahashi Shrine (Q11670742)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QTakahashi Shrine (Q11670742)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Takahashi Shrine (Q11670742)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

In {{ill|lt=684|Q31128|WD=Q31128}} ({{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181}} 12), descendants of Iwaka Mutsukari who ruled this area were granted the surname Takahashi Ason, and in that year they merged their ancestral deity Iwaka Mutsukari at this shrine and called it Takahashi Shrine. In {{ill|lt=1029|Q19274|WD=Q19274}} ({{ill|lt=Chōgen|Q1193557|WD=Q1193557}} 2), when a well was dug in the precincts, a large {{ill|lt=carp|Q81110|WD=Q81110}} appeared. Reported to the capital, {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Ichijō|Q374087|WD=Q374087}} deemed it truly miraculous and granted the imperial plaque "Japan’s sole carp-forbidden shrine." Since then, parishioners do not eat carp, do not use vessels painted with carp, and do not raise {{ill|lt=koinobori|Q779745|WD=Q779745}} in May. From this comes the alternate name '''Carp Myōjin'''. In the medieval period it was protected by the {{ill|lt=Yūki clan|Q2975399|WD=Q2975399}} of {{ill|lt=Shimōsa Province|Q1053695|WD=Q1053695}}. More than 20 documents remain, including donation letters, prayers, and practice records from castle lords {{ill|lt=Yūki Masaasahi|Q11606587|WD=Q11606587}}, {{ill|lt=Yūki Masanao|Q11606589|WD=Q11606589}}, {{ill|lt=Yūki Masakatsu|Q8062739|WD=Q8062739}}, {{ill|lt=Yūki Harutomo|Q8062736|WD=Q8062736}}, and {{ill|lt=Yūki Hideyasu|Q1130929|WD=Q1130929}}. Even after Hideyasu was transferred to Echizen Fukui, ceremonial proxy visits were maintained generation after generation until the Meiji Restoration. ===Cultural properties=== === Tochigi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property === * Rōmon - Donated by the Mizuno family, lords of Yūki Castle; construction took 16 years from Hōreki 4 to Meiwa 7 ({{ill|lt=1770|Q7683|WD=Q7683}}). Restoration was carried out from 2017 to 2018. === Oyama City designated intangible cultural property === * Daidai Kagura - dedicated at the autumn annual festival. === Chinowa crossing === The chinowa ring at Takahashi Shrine has a rare square shape, about 2 m per side, not circular. The reason is unknown. It is made with one-sided reed as core, wrapped in makomo and bound with cogon-grass ropeYomiuri Shimbun Tochigi edition, August 3, 2017, p.29.. === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === * Kōten Kōkyūjo / National Priests’ Association corrected edition [{{NDLDC|1442211}} ''Engishiki, upper volume'' p.319] (Ōokayama Shoten, 1929) - Modern Digital Library ===External links=== *[https://www.facebook.com/TAKAHASHIJINJA Engishiki Takahashi Shrine] (official site) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかはししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takahashi Shrine (Q11670742)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takahashi Shrine (Q11670742)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Takahashi Shrine (Q11670742)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:栃木県の神社]] [[Category:下総国の式内社]] [[Category:栃木県の旧県社]] [[Category:小山市の歴史]] [[Category:小山市の建築物]] [[category:日本の食文化|神たかはししんしや]] [[Category:高橋氏|社たかはししんしや]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QTakahashi Shrine (Q11670742)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QTakahashi Shrine (Q11670742)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Takahashi Shrine (Q11670742)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Amakagami-no-Mikoto
Amayorozu-no-Mikoto | 別名=Carp Myōjin, Takahashi Myōjin | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}（small）・former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} | 創建=(trad.) {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723}} 41 ({{ill|lt=111|Q30640|WD=Q30640}}) | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | 例祭=First Saturday of October | 神事=Dedication of daidai-kagura | 地図={{Location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Takahashi Shrine|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250}} }} '''Takahashi Shrine''' (たかはしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Oyama|Q374511|WD=Q374511}}, {{ill|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|Q44843|WD=Q44843}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was prefectural shrine. It is the oldest shrine whose main deity is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Iwaka Mutsukari no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, revered as the "ancestral deity of {{ill|lt=cuisine|Q1778821|WD=Q1778821}}." Associated deities are Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto, Amakagami-no-Mikoto, Amayorozu-no-Mikoto, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takaokami-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Homusubi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyouke-hime-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}. It has four branch shrines in {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277}} and is worshiped by people in culinary professions. * Hida Takahashi Shrine ({{ill|lt=Gero Onsen Gassho Village|Q11360703|WD=Q11360703}}) * Keihoku Takahashi Shrine ({{ill|lt=Yoake no Mori|Q17209177|WD=Q17209177}} in Nakatsugawa) * Kane Takahashi Shrine (inside Kane Shrine in Gifu) * Hida Takayama Takahashi Shrine (inside Hie Shrine in Takayama) ===History=== According to shrine tradition, in {{ill|lt=111|Q30640|WD=Q30640}} (Keikō 41), when {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258}} campaigned eastward, he invited Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto, Amakagami-no-Mikoto, and Amayorozu-no-Mikoto here and prayed for victory. When Emperor Keikō later inspected the campaign sites, Iwaka Mutsukari no Mikoto, of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kashiwade no Omi|1=UNKNOWN}} line, was old and with imperial permission remained here. In {{ill|lt=684|Q31128|WD=Q31128}} ({{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181}} 12), descendants of Iwaka Mutsukari who ruled this area were granted the surname Takahashi Ason, and in that year they merged their ancestral deity Iwaka Mutsukari at this shrine and called it Takahashi Shrine. In {{ill|lt=1029|Q19274|WD=Q19274}} ({{ill|lt=Chōgen|Q1193557|WD=Q1193557}} 2), when a well was dug in the precincts, a large {{ill|lt=carp|Q81110|WD=Q81110}} appeared. Reported to the capital, {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Ichijō|Q374087|WD=Q374087}} deemed it truly miraculous and granted the imperial plaque "Japan’s sole carp-forbidden shrine." Since then, parishioners do not eat carp, do not use vessels painted with carp, and do not raise {{ill|lt=koinobori|Q779745|WD=Q779745}} in May. From this comes the alternate name '''Carp Myōjin'''. In the medieval period it was protected by the {{ill|lt=Yūki clan|Q2975399|WD=Q2975399}} of {{ill|lt=Shimōsa Province|Q1053695|WD=Q1053695}}. More than 20 documents remain, including donation letters, prayers, and practice records from castle lords {{ill|lt=Yūki Masaasahi|Q11606587|WD=Q11606587}}, {{ill|lt=Yūki Masanao|Q11606589|WD=Q11606589}}, {{ill|lt=Yūki Masakatsu|Q8062739|WD=Q8062739}}, {{ill|lt=Yūki Harutomo|Q8062736|WD=Q8062736}}, and {{ill|lt=Yūki Hideyasu|Q1130929|WD=Q1130929}}. Even after Hideyasu was transferred to Echizen Fukui, ceremonial proxy visits were maintained generation after generation until the Meiji Restoration. ===Cultural properties=== === Tochigi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property === * Rōmon - Donated by the Mizuno family, lords of Yūki Castle; construction took 16 years from Hōreki 4 to Meiwa 7 ({{ill|lt=1770|Q7683|WD=Q7683}}). Restoration was carried out from 2017 to 2018. === Oyama City designated intangible cultural property === * Daidai Kagura - dedicated at the autumn annual festival. === Chinowa crossing === The chinowa ring at Takahashi Shrine has a rare square shape, about 2 m per side, not circular. The reason is unknown. It is made with one-sided reed as core, wrapped in makomo and bound with cogon-grass ropeYomiuri Shimbun Tochigi edition, August 3, 2017, p.29.. === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === * Kōten Kōkyūjo / National Priests’ Association corrected edition [{{NDLDC|1442211}} ''Engishiki, upper volume'' p.319] (Ōokayama Shoten, 1929) - Modern Digital Library ===External links=== *[https://www.facebook.com/TAKAHASHIJINJA Engishiki Takahashi Shrine] (official site) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかはししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takahashi Shrine (Q11670742)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takahashi Shrine (Q11670742)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Takahashi Shrine (Q11670742)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:栃木県の神社]] [[Category:下総国の式内社]] [[Category:栃木県の旧県社]] [[Category:小山市の歴史]] [[Category:小山市の建築物]] [[category:日本の食文化|神たかはししんしや]] [[Category:高橋氏|社たかはししんしや]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QTakahashi Shrine (Q11670742)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QTakahashi Shrine (Q11670742)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Takahashi Shrine (Q11670742)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was prefectural shrine. It is the oldest shrine whose main deity is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Iwaka Mutsukari no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, revered as the "ancestral deity of {{ill|lt=cuisine|Q1778821|WD=Q1778821}}." Associated deities are Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto, Amakagami-no-Mikoto, Amayorozu-no-Mikoto, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takaokami-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Homusubi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyouke-hime-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}. It has four branch shrines in {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277}} and is worshiped by people in culinary professions. * Hida Takahashi Shrine ({{ill|lt=Gero Onsen Gassho Village|Q11360703|WD=Q11360703}}) * Keihoku Takahashi Shrine ({{ill|lt=Yoake no Mori|Q17209177|WD=Q17209177}} in Nakatsugawa) * Kane Takahashi Shrine (inside Kane Shrine in Gifu) * Hida Takayama Takahashi Shrine (inside Hie Shrine in Takayama) ===History=== According to shrine tradition, in {{ill|lt=111|Q30640|WD=Q30640}} (Keikō 41), when {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258}} campaigned eastward, he invited Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto, Amakagami-no-Mikoto, and Amayorozu-no-Mikoto here and prayed for victory. When Emperor Keikō later inspected the campaign sites, Iwaka Mutsukari no Mikoto, of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kashiwade no Omi|1=UNKNOWN}} line, was old and with imperial permission remained here. In {{ill|lt=684|Q31128|WD=Q31128}} ({{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181}} 12), descendants of Iwaka Mutsukari who ruled this area were granted the surname Takahashi Ason, and in that year they merged their ancestral deity Iwaka Mutsukari at this shrine and called it Takahashi Shrine. In {{ill|lt=1029|Q19274|WD=Q19274}} ({{ill|lt=Chōgen|Q1193557|WD=Q1193557}} 2), when a well was dug in the precincts, a large {{ill|lt=carp|Q81110|WD=Q81110}} appeared. Reported to the capital, {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Ichijō|Q374087|WD=Q374087}} deemed it truly miraculous and granted the imperial plaque "Japan’s sole carp-forbidden shrine." Since then, parishioners do not eat carp, do not use vessels painted with carp, and do not raise {{ill|lt=koinobori|Q779745|WD=Q779745}} in May. From this comes the alternate name '''Carp Myōjin'''. In the medieval period it was protected by the {{ill|lt=Yūki clan|Q2975399|WD=Q2975399}} of {{ill|lt=Shimōsa Province|Q1053695|WD=Q1053695}}. More than 20 documents remain, including donation letters, prayers, and practice records from castle lords {{ill|lt=Yūki Masaasahi|Q11606587|WD=Q11606587}}, {{ill|lt=Yūki Masanao|Q11606589|WD=Q11606589}}, {{ill|lt=Yūki Masakatsu|Q8062739|WD=Q8062739}}, {{ill|lt=Yūki Harutomo|Q8062736|WD=Q8062736}}, and {{ill|lt=Yūki Hideyasu|Q1130929|WD=Q1130929}}. Even after Hideyasu was transferred to Echizen Fukui, ceremonial proxy visits were maintained generation after generation until the Meiji Restoration. ===Cultural properties=== === Tochigi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property === * Rōmon - Donated by the Mizuno family, lords of Yūki Castle; construction took 16 years from Hōreki 4 to Meiwa 7 ({{ill|lt=1770|Q7683|WD=Q7683}}). Restoration was carried out from 2017 to 2018. === Oyama City designated intangible cultural property === * Daidai Kagura - dedicated at the autumn annual festival. === Chinowa crossing === The chinowa ring at Takahashi Shrine has a rare square shape, about 2 m per side, not circular. The reason is unknown. It is made with one-sided reed as core, wrapped in makomo and bound with cogon-grass ropeYomiuri Shimbun Tochigi edition, August 3, 2017, p.29.. === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === * Kōten Kōkyūjo / National Priests’ Association corrected edition [{{NDLDC|1442211}} ''Engishiki, upper volume'' p.319] (Ōokayama Shoten, 1929) - Modern Digital Library ===External links=== *[https://www.facebook.com/TAKAHASHIJINJA Engishiki Takahashi Shrine] (official site) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかはししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takahashi Shrine (Q11670742)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takahashi Shrine (Q11670742)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Takahashi Shrine (Q11670742)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:栃木県の神社]] [[Category:下総国の式内社]] [[Category:栃木県の旧県社]] [[Category:小山市の歴史]] [[Category:小山市の建築物]] [[category:日本の食文化|神たかはししんしや]] [[Category:高橋氏|社たかはししんしや]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QTakahashi Shrine (Q11670742)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QTakahashi Shrine (Q11670742)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Takahashi Shrine (Q11670742)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

[[JA:高椅神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11670742}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2014年7月}} {{Otheruses||{{ill|lt=Takahashi Shrine (Mishima)|Q11670742|WD=Q11670742}} in {{ill|lt=Mishima|Q653478|WD=Q653478}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}}} {{神社 | 名称=Takahashi Shrine | 画像=[[File:高椅神社楼門.jpg|270px|Takahashi Shrine rōmon]]
Rōmon (Tochigi Prefecture designated cultural property) | 所在地={{ill|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|Q44843|WD=Q44843}}{{ill|lt=Oyama|Q374511|WD=Q374511}} Takahashi 702 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Iwaka Mutsukari no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
Amakagami-no-Mikoto
Amayorozu-no-Mikoto | 別名=Carp Myōjin, Takahashi Myōjin | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}（small）・former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}} | 創建=(trad.) {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723}} 41 ({{ill|lt=111|Q30640|WD=Q30640}}) | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | 例祭=First Saturday of October | 神事=Dedication of daidai-kagura | 地図={{Location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Takahashi Shrine|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250}} }} '''Takahashi Shrine''' (たかはしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Oyama|Q374511|WD=Q374511}}, {{ill|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|Q44843|WD=Q44843}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was prefectural shrine. It is the oldest shrine whose main deity is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Iwaka Mutsukari no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, revered as the "ancestral deity of {{ill|lt=cuisine|Q1778821|WD=Q1778821}}." Associated deities are Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto, Amakagami-no-Mikoto, Amayorozu-no-Mikoto, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takaokami-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Homusubi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyouke-hime-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}. It has four branch shrines in {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277}} and is worshiped by people in culinary professions. * Hida Takahashi Shrine ({{ill|lt=Gero Onsen Gassho Village|Q11360703|WD=Q11360703}}) * Keihoku Takahashi Shrine ({{ill|lt=Yoake no Mori|Q17209177|WD=Q17209177}} in Nakatsugawa) * Kane Takahashi Shrine (inside Kane Shrine in Gifu) * Hida Takayama Takahashi Shrine (inside Hie Shrine in Takayama) ===History=== According to shrine tradition, in {{ill|lt=111|Q30640|WD=Q30640}} (Keikō 41), when {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258}} campaigned eastward, he invited Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto, Amakagami-no-Mikoto, and Amayorozu-no-Mikoto here and prayed for victory. When Emperor Keikō later inspected the campaign sites, Iwaka Mutsukari no Mikoto, of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kashiwade no Omi|1=UNKNOWN}} line, was old and with imperial permission remained here. In {{ill|lt=684|Q31128|WD=Q31128}} ({{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181}} 12), descendants of Iwaka Mutsukari who ruled this area were granted the surname Takahashi Ason, and in that year they merged their ancestral deity Iwaka Mutsukari at this shrine and called it Takahashi Shrine. In {{ill|lt=1029|Q19274|WD=Q19274}} ({{ill|lt=Chōgen|Q1193557|WD=Q1193557}} 2), when a well was dug in the precincts, a large {{ill|lt=carp|Q81110|WD=Q81110}} appeared. Reported to the capital, {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Ichijō|Q374087|WD=Q374087}} deemed it truly miraculous and granted the imperial plaque "Japan’s sole carp-forbidden shrine." Since then, parishioners do not eat carp, do not use vessels painted with carp, and do not raise {{ill|lt=koinobori|Q779745|WD=Q779745}} in May. From this comes the alternate name '''Carp Myōjin'''. In the medieval period it was protected by the {{ill|lt=Yūki clan|Q2975399|WD=Q2975399}} of {{ill|lt=Shimōsa Province|Q1053695|WD=Q1053695}}. More than 20 documents remain, including donation letters, prayers, and practice records from castle lords {{ill|lt=Yūki Masaasahi|Q11606587|WD=Q11606587}}, {{ill|lt=Yūki Masanao|Q11606589|WD=Q11606589}}, {{ill|lt=Yūki Masakatsu|Q8062739|WD=Q8062739}}, {{ill|lt=Yūki Harutomo|Q8062736|WD=Q8062736}}, and {{ill|lt=Yūki Hideyasu|Q1130929|WD=Q1130929}}. Even after Hideyasu was transferred to Echizen Fukui, ceremonial proxy visits were maintained generation after generation until the Meiji Restoration. ===Cultural properties=== === Tochigi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property === * Rōmon - Donated by the Mizuno family, lords of Yūki Castle; construction took 16 years from Hōreki 4 to Meiwa 7 ({{ill|lt=1770|Q7683|WD=Q7683}}). Restoration was carried out from 2017 to 2018. === Oyama City designated intangible cultural property === * Daidai Kagura - dedicated at the autumn annual festival. === Chinowa crossing === The chinowa ring at Takahashi Shrine has a rare square shape, about 2 m per side, not circular. The reason is unknown. It is made with one-sided reed as core, wrapped in makomo and bound with cogon-grass ropeYomiuri Shimbun Tochigi edition, August 3, 2017, p.29.. === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === * Kōten Kōkyūjo / National Priests’ Association corrected edition [{{NDLDC|1442211}} ''Engishiki, upper volume'' p.319] (Ōokayama Shoten, 1929) - Modern Digital Library ===External links=== *[https://www.facebook.com/TAKAHASHIJINJA Engishiki Takahashi Shrine] (official site) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかはししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takahashi Shrine (Q11670742)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takahashi Shrine (Q11670742)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Takahashi Shrine (Q11670742)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:栃木県の神社]] [[Category:下総国の式内社]] [[Category:栃木県の旧県社]] [[Category:小山市の歴史]] [[Category:小山市の建築物]] [[category:日本の食文化|神たかはししんしや]] [[Category:高橋氏|社たかはししんしや]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QTakahashi Shrine (Q11670742)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QTakahashi Shrine (Q11670742)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Takahashi Shrine (Q11670742)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

[[JA:高椅神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11670742}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2014年7月}} {{Otheruses||{{ill|lt=Takahashi Shrine (Mishima)|Q11670742|WD=Q11670742}} in {{ill|lt=Mishima|Q653478|WD=Q653478}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}}} {{神社 | 名称=Takahashi Shrine | 画像=[[File:高椅神社楼門.jpg|270px|Takahashi Shrine rōmon]]
Rōmon (Tochigi Prefecture designated cultural property) | 所在地={{ill|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|Q44843|WD=Q44843}}{{ill|lt=Oyama|Q374511|WD=Q374511}} Takahashi 702 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Iwaka Mutsukari no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q478504|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
Amakagami-no-Mikoto
Amayorozu-no-Mikoto | 別名=Carp Myōjin, Takahashi Myōjin | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}（small）・former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}} | 創建=(trad.) {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723}} 41 ({{ill|lt=111|Q30640|WD=Q30640}}) | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | 例祭=First Saturday of October | 神事=Dedication of daidai-kagura | 地図={{Location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Takahashi Shrine|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250}} }} '''Takahashi Shrine''' (たかはしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Oyama|Q374511|WD=Q374511}}, {{ill|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|Q44843|WD=Q44843}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was prefectural shrine. It is the oldest shrine whose main deity is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Iwaka Mutsukari no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, revered as the "ancestral deity of {{ill|lt=cuisine|Q1778821|WD=Q1778821}}." Associated deities are Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto, Amakagami-no-Mikoto, Amayorozu-no-Mikoto, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takaokami-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Homusubi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyouke-hime-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}. It has four branch shrines in {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277}} and is worshiped by people in culinary professions. * Hida Takahashi Shrine ({{ill|lt=Gero Onsen Gassho Village|Q11360703|WD=Q11360703}}) * Keihoku Takahashi Shrine ({{ill|lt=Yoake no Mori|Q17209177|WD=Q17209177}} in Nakatsugawa) * Kane Takahashi Shrine (inside Kane Shrine in Gifu) * Hida Takayama Takahashi Shrine (inside Hie Shrine in Takayama) ===History=== According to shrine tradition, in {{ill|lt=111|Q30640|WD=Q30640}} (Keikō 41), when {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258}} campaigned eastward, he invited Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto, Amakagami-no-Mikoto, and Amayorozu-no-Mikoto here and prayed for victory. When Emperor Keikō later inspected the campaign sites, Iwaka Mutsukari no Mikoto, of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kashiwade no Omi|1=UNKNOWN}} line, was old and with imperial permission remained here. In {{ill|lt=684|Q31128|WD=Q31128}} ({{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181}} 12), descendants of Iwaka Mutsukari who ruled this area were granted the surname Takahashi Ason, and in that year they merged their ancestral deity Iwaka Mutsukari at this shrine and called it Takahashi Shrine. In {{ill|lt=1029|Q19274|WD=Q19274}} ({{ill|lt=Chōgen|Q1193557|WD=Q1193557}} 2), when a well was dug in the precincts, a large {{ill|lt=carp|Q81110|WD=Q81110}} appeared. Reported to the capital, {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Ichijō|Q374087|WD=Q374087}} deemed it truly miraculous and granted the imperial plaque "Japan’s sole carp-forbidden shrine." Since then, parishioners do not eat carp, do not use vessels painted with carp, and do not raise {{ill|lt=koinobori|Q779745|WD=Q779745}} in May. From this comes the alternate name '''Carp Myōjin'''. In the medieval period it was protected by the {{ill|lt=Yūki clan|Q2975399|WD=Q2975399}} of {{ill|lt=Shimōsa Province|Q1053695|WD=Q1053695}}. More than 20 documents remain, including donation letters, prayers, and practice records from castle lords {{ill|lt=Yūki Masaasahi|Q11606587|WD=Q11606587}}, {{ill|lt=Yūki Masanao|Q11606589|WD=Q11606589}}, {{ill|lt=Yūki Masakatsu|Q8062739|WD=Q8062739}}, {{ill|lt=Yūki Harutomo|Q8062736|WD=Q8062736}}, and {{ill|lt=Yūki Hideyasu|Q1130929|WD=Q1130929}}. Even after Hideyasu was transferred to Echizen Fukui, ceremonial proxy visits were maintained generation after generation until the Meiji Restoration. ===Cultural properties=== === Tochigi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property === * Rōmon - Donated by the Mizuno family, lords of Yūki Castle; construction took 16 years from Hōreki 4 to Meiwa 7 ({{ill|lt=1770|Q7683|WD=Q7683}}). Restoration was carried out from 2017 to 2018. === Oyama City designated intangible cultural property === * Daidai Kagura - dedicated at the autumn annual festival. === Chinowa crossing === The chinowa ring at Takahashi Shrine has a rare square shape, about 2 m per side, not circular. The reason is unknown. It is made with one-sided reed as core, wrapped in makomo and bound with cogon-grass ropeYomiuri Shimbun Tochigi edition, August 3, 2017, p.29.. === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === * Kōten Kōkyūjo / National Priests’ Association corrected edition [{{NDLDC|1442211}} ''Engishiki, upper volume'' p.319] (Ōokayama Shoten, 1929) - Modern Digital Library ===External links=== *[https://www.facebook.com/TAKAHASHIJINJA Engishiki Takahashi Shrine] (official site) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかはししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takahashi Shrine (Q11670742)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takahashi Shrine (Q11670742)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Takahashi Shrine (Q11670742)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:栃木県の神社]] [[Category:下総国の式内社]] [[Category:栃木県の旧県社]] [[Category:小山市の歴史]] [[Category:小山市の建築物]] [[category:日本の食文化|神たかはししんしや]] [[Category:高橋氏|社たかはししんしや]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QTakahashi Shrine (Q11670742)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QTakahashi Shrine (Q11670742)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Takahashi Shrine (Q11670742)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

[[JA:高椅神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11670742}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2014年7月}} {{Otheruses||{{ill|lt=Takahashi Shrine (Mishima)|Q11670742|WD=Q11670742|1=Takahashi Shrine (Q11670742)}} in {{ill|lt=Mishima|Q653478|WD=Q653478|1=Mishima, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}}} {{神社 | 名称=Takahashi Shrine | 画像=[[File:高椅神社楼門.jpg|270px|Takahashi Shrine rōmon]]
Rōmon (Tochigi Prefecture designated cultural property) | 所在地={{ill|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|Q44843|WD=Q44843|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Oyama|Q374511|WD=Q374511|1=Oyama, Tochigi}} Takahashi 702 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Iwaka Mutsukari no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q478504|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}}
Amakagami-no-Mikoto
Amayorozu-no-Mikoto | 別名=Carp Myōjin, Takahashi Myōjin | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）・former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} | 創建=(trad.) {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} 41 ({{ill|lt=111|Q30640|WD=Q30640|1=AD 111}}) | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | 例祭=First Saturday of October | 神事=Dedication of daidai-kagura | 地図={{Location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Takahashi Shrine|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250}} }} '''Takahashi Shrine''' (たかはしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Oyama|Q374511|WD=Q374511|1=Oyama, Tochigi}}, {{ill|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|Q44843|WD=Q44843|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was prefectural shrine. It is the oldest shrine whose main deity is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Iwaka Mutsukari no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, revered as the "ancestral deity of {{ill|lt=cuisine|Q1778821|WD=Q1778821|1=Cuisine}}." Associated deities are Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto, Amakagami-no-Mikoto, Amayorozu-no-Mikoto, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|1=Futsunushi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takaokami-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Homusubi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyouke-hime-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}. It has four branch shrines in {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}} and is worshiped by people in culinary professions. * Hida Takahashi Shrine ({{ill|lt=Gero Onsen Gassho Village|Q11360703|WD=Q11360703|1=Gero Onsen Gasshō-mura}}) * Keihoku Takahashi Shrine ({{ill|lt=Yoake no Mori|Q17209177|WD=Q17209177|1=Yoake no Mori}} in Nakatsugawa) * Kane Takahashi Shrine (inside Kane Shrine in Gifu) * Hida Takayama Takahashi Shrine (inside Hie Shrine in Takayama) ===History=== According to shrine tradition, in {{ill|lt=111|Q30640|WD=Q30640|1=AD 111}} (Keikō 41), when {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|1=Yamato Takeru}} campaigned eastward, he invited Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto, Amakagami-no-Mikoto, and Amayorozu-no-Mikoto here and prayed for victory. When Emperor Keikō later inspected the campaign sites, Iwaka Mutsukari no Mikoto, of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kashiwade no Omi|1=UNKNOWN}} line, was old and with imperial permission remained here. In {{ill|lt=684|Q31128|WD=Q31128|1=684}} ({{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}} 12), descendants of Iwaka Mutsukari who ruled this area were granted the surname Takahashi Ason, and in that year they merged their ancestral deity Iwaka Mutsukari at this shrine and called it Takahashi Shrine. In {{ill|lt=1029|Q19274|WD=Q19274|1=1029}} ({{ill|lt=Chōgen|Q1193557|WD=Q1193557|1=Chōgen}} 2), when a well was dug in the precincts, a large {{ill|lt=carp|Q81110|WD=Q81110|1=Common carp}} appeared. Reported to the capital, {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Ichijō|Q374087|WD=Q374087|1=Emperor Go-Ichijō}} deemed it truly miraculous and granted the imperial plaque "Japan’s sole carp-forbidden shrine." Since then, parishioners do not eat carp, do not use vessels painted with carp, and do not raise {{ill|lt=koinobori|Q779745|WD=Q779745|1=Koinobori}} in May. From this comes the alternate name '''Carp Myōjin'''. In the medieval period it was protected by the {{ill|lt=Yūki clan|Q2975399|WD=Q2975399|1=Yūki clan}} of {{ill|lt=Shimōsa Province|Q1053695|WD=Q1053695|1=Shimōsa Province}}. More than 20 documents remain, including donation letters, prayers, and practice records from castle lords {{ill|lt=Yūki Masaasahi|Q11606587|WD=Q11606587|1=Yūki Masaasahi}}, {{ill|lt=Yūki Masanao|Q11606589|WD=Q11606589|1=Yūki Masanao}}, {{ill|lt=Yūki Masakatsu|Q8062739|WD=Q8062739|1=Yūki Masakatsu}}, {{ill|lt=Yūki Harutomo|Q8062736|WD=Q8062736|1=Yūki Harutomo}}, and {{ill|lt=Yūki Hideyasu|Q1130929|WD=Q1130929|1=Yūki Hideyasu}}. Even after Hideyasu was transferred to Echizen Fukui, ceremonial proxy visits were maintained generation after generation until the Meiji Restoration. ===Cultural properties=== === Tochigi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property === * Rōmon - Donated by the Mizuno family, lords of Yūki Castle; construction took 16 years from Hōreki 4 to Meiwa 7 ({{ill|lt=1770|Q7683|WD=Q7683|1=1770}}). Restoration was carried out from 2017 to 2018. === Oyama City designated intangible cultural property === * Daidai Kagura - dedicated at the autumn annual festival. === Chinowa crossing === The chinowa ring at Takahashi Shrine has a rare square shape, about 2 m per side, not circular. The reason is unknown. It is made with one-sided reed as core, wrapped in makomo and bound with cogon-grass ropeYomiuri Shimbun Tochigi edition, August 3, 2017, p.29.. === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === * Kōten Kōkyūjo / National Priests’ Association corrected edition [{{NDLDC|1442211}} ''Engishiki, upper volume'' p.319] (Ōokayama Shoten, 1929) - Modern Digital Library ===External links=== *[https://www.facebook.com/TAKAHASHIJINJA Engishiki Takahashi Shrine] (official site) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかはししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takahashi Shrine (Q11670742)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takahashi Shrine (Q11670742)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Takahashi Shrine (Q11670742)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:栃木県の神社]] [[Category:下総国の式内社]] [[Category:栃木県の旧県社]] [[Category:小山市の歴史]] [[Category:小山市の建築物]] [[category:日本の食文化|神たかはししんしや]] [[Category:高橋氏|社たかはししんしや]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QTakahashi Shrine (Q11670742)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QTakahashi Shrine (Q11670742)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Takahashi Shrine (Q11670742)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

It has four branch shrines in {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}} and is worshiped by people in culinary professions. * Hida Takahashi Shrine ({{ill|lt=Gero Onsen Gassho Village|Q11360703|WD=Q11360703|1=Gero Onsen Gasshō-mura}}) * Keihoku Takahashi Shrine ({{ill|lt=Yoake no Mori|Q17209177|WD=Q17209177|1=Yoake no Mori}} in Nakatsugawa) * Kane Takahashi Shrine (inside Kane Shrine in Gifu) * Hida Takayama Takahashi Shrine (inside Hie Shrine in Takayama) ===History=== According to shrine tradition, in {{ill|lt=111|Q30640|WD=Q30640|1=AD 111}} (Keikō 41), when {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|1=Yamato Takeru}} campaigned eastward, he invited Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto, Amakagami-no-Mikoto, and Amayorozu-no-Mikoto here and prayed for victory. When Emperor Keikō later inspected the campaign sites, Iwaka Mutsukari no Mikoto, of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kashiwade no Omi|1=UNKNOWN}} line, was old and with imperial permission remained here. In {{ill|lt=684|Q31128|WD=Q31128|1=684}} ({{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}} 12), descendants of Iwaka Mutsukari who ruled this area were granted the surname Takahashi Ason, and in that year they merged their ancestral deity Iwaka Mutsukari at this shrine and called it Takahashi Shrine. In {{ill|lt=1029|Q19274|WD=Q19274|1=1029}} ({{ill|lt=Chōgen|Q1193557|WD=Q1193557|1=Chōgen}} 2), when a well was dug in the precincts, a large {{ill|lt=carp|Q81110|WD=Q81110|1=Common carp}} appeared. Reported to the capital, {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Ichijō|Q374087|WD=Q374087|1=Emperor Go-Ichijō}} deemed it truly miraculous and granted the imperial plaque "Japan’s sole carp-forbidden shrine." Since then, parishioners do not eat carp, do not use vessels painted with carp, and do not raise {{ill|lt=koinobori|Q779745|WD=Q779745|1=Koinobori}} in May. From this comes the alternate name '''Carp Myōjin'''. In the medieval period it was protected by the {{ill|lt=Yūki clan|Q2975399|WD=Q2975399|1=Yūki clan}} of {{ill|lt=Shimōsa Province|Q1053695|WD=Q1053695|1=Shimōsa Province}}. More than 20 documents remain, including donation letters, prayers, and practice records from castle lords {{ill|lt=Yūki Masaasahi|Q11606587|WD=Q11606587|1=Yūki Masaasahi}}, {{ill|lt=Yūki Masanao|Q11606589|WD=Q11606589|1=Yūki Masanao}}, {{ill|lt=Yūki Masakatsu|Q8062739|WD=Q8062739|1=Yūki Masakatsu}}, {{ill|lt=Yūki Harutomo|Q8062736|WD=Q8062736|1=Yūki Harutomo}}, and {{ill|lt=Yūki Hideyasu|Q1130929|WD=Q1130929|1=Yūki Hideyasu}}. Even after Hideyasu was transferred to Echizen Fukui, ceremonial proxy visits were maintained generation after generation until the Meiji Restoration. ===Cultural properties=== === Tochigi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property === * Rōmon - Donated by the Mizuno family, lords of Yūki Castle; construction took 16 years from Hōreki 4 to Meiwa 7 ({{ill|lt=1770|Q7683|WD=Q7683|1=1770}}). Restoration was carried out from 2017 to 2018. === Oyama City designated intangible cultural property === * Daidai Kagura - dedicated at the autumn annual festival. === Chinowa crossing === The chinowa ring at Takahashi Shrine has a rare square shape, about 2 m per side, not circular. The reason is unknown. It is made with one-sided reed as core, wrapped in makomo and bound with cogon-grass ropeYomiuri Shimbun Tochigi edition, August 3, 2017, p.29.. === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === * Kōten Kōkyūjo / National Priests’ Association corrected edition [{{NDLDC|1442211}} ''Engishiki, upper volume'' p.319] (Ōokayama Shoten, 1929) - Modern Digital Library ===External links=== *[https://www.facebook.com/TAKAHASHIJINJA Engishiki Takahashi Shrine] (official site) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかはししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takahashi Shrine (Q11670742)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takahashi Shrine (Q11670742)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Takahashi Shrine (Q11670742)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:栃木県の神社]] [[Category:下総国の式内社]] [[Category:栃木県の旧県社]] [[Category:小山市の歴史]] [[Category:小山市の建築物]] [[category:日本の食文化|神たかはししんしや]] [[Category:高橋氏|社たかはししんしや]] {{DEFAULTSORT:QTakahashi Shrine (Q11670742)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QTakahashi Shrine (Q11670742)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Takahashi Shrine (Q11670742)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

[[JA:高椅神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11670742}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2014年7月}} {{Otheruses||{{ill|lt=Takahashi Shrine (Mishima)|Q11670742|WD=Q11670742|1=Takahashi Shrine (Q11670742)}} in {{ill|lt=Mishima|Q653478|WD=Q653478|1=Mishima, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}}} {{神社 | 名称=Takahashi Shrine | 画像=[[File:高椅神社楼門.jpg|270px|Takahashi Shrine rōmon]]
Rōmon (Tochigi Prefecture designated cultural property) | 所在地={{ill|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|Q44843|WD=Q44843|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Oyama|Q374511|WD=Q374511|1=Oyama, Tochigi}} Takahashi 702 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Iwaka Mutsukari no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q478504|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}}
Amakagami-no-Mikoto
Amayorozu-no-Mikoto | 別名=Carp Myōjin, Takahashi Myōjin | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）・former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} | 創建=(trad.) {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} 41 ({{ill|lt=111|Q30640|WD=Q30640|1=AD 111}}) | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | 例祭=First Saturday of October | 神事=Dedication of daidai-kagura | 地図={{Location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Takahashi Shrine|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250}} }} '''Takahashi Shrine''' (たかはしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Oyama|Q374511|WD=Q374511|1=Oyama, Tochigi}}, {{ill|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|Q44843|WD=Q44843|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was prefectural shrine. It is the oldest shrine whose main deity is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Iwaka Mutsukari no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, revered as the "ancestral deity of {{ill|lt=cuisine|Q1778821|WD=Q1778821|1=Cuisine}}." Associated deities are Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto, Amakagami-no-Mikoto, Amayorozu-no-Mikoto, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|1=Futsunushi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takaokami-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Homusubi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyouke-hime-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}. It has four branch shrines in {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}} and is worshiped by people in culinary professions. * Hida Takahashi Shrine ({{ill|lt=Gero Onsen Gassho Village|Q11360703|WD=Q11360703|1=Gero Onsen Gasshō-mura}}) * Keihoku Takahashi Shrine ({{ill|lt=Yoake no Mori|Q17209177|WD=Q17209177|1=Yoake no Mori}} in Nakatsugawa) * Kane Takahashi Shrine (inside Kane Shrine in Gifu) * Hida Takayama Takahashi Shrine (inside Hie Shrine in Takayama) ===History=== According to shrine tradition, in {{ill|lt=111|Q30640|WD=Q30640|1=AD 111}} (Keikō 41), when {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|1=Yamato Takeru}} campaigned eastward, he invited Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto, Amakagami-no-Mikoto, and Amayorozu-no-Mikoto here and prayed for victory. When Emperor Keikō later inspected the campaign sites, Iwaka Mutsukari no Mikoto, of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kashiwade no Omi|1=UNKNOWN}} line, was old and with imperial permission remained here. In {{ill|lt=684|Q31128|WD=Q31128|1=684}} ({{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}} 12), descendants of Iwaka Mutsukari who ruled this area were granted the surname Takahashi Ason, and in that year they merged their ancestral deity Iwaka Mutsukari at this shrine and called it Takahashi Shrine. In {{ill|lt=1029|Q19274|WD=Q19274|1=1029}} ({{ill|lt=Chōgen|Q1193557|WD=Q1193557|1=Chōgen}} 2), when a well was dug in the precincts, a large {{ill|lt=carp|Q81110|WD=Q81110|1=Common carp}} appeared. Reported to the capital, {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Ichijō|Q374087|WD=Q374087|1=Emperor Go-Ichijō}} deemed it truly miraculous and granted the imperial plaque "Japan’s sole carp-forbidden shrine." Since then, parishioners do not eat carp, do not use vessels painted with carp, and do not raise {{ill|lt=koinobori|Q779745|WD=Q779745|1=Koinobori}} in May. From this comes the alternate name '''Carp Myōjin'''. In the medieval period it was protected by the {{ill|lt=Yūki clan|Q2975399|WD=Q2975399|1=Yūki clan}} of {{ill|lt=Shimōsa Province|Q1053695|WD=Q1053695|1=Shimōsa Province}}. More than 20 documents remain, including donation letters, prayers, and practice records from castle lords {{ill|lt=Yūki Masaasahi|Q11606587|WD=Q11606587|1=Yūki Masaasahi}}, {{ill|lt=Yūki Masanao|Q11606589|WD=Q11606589|1=Yūki Masanao}}, {{ill|lt=Yūki Masakatsu|Q8062739|WD=Q8062739|1=Yūki Masakatsu}}, {{ill|lt=Yūki Harutomo|Q8062736|WD=Q8062736|1=Yūki Harutomo}}, and {{ill|lt=Yūki Hideyasu|Q1130929|WD=Q1130929|1=Yūki Hideyasu}}. Even after Hideyasu was transferred to Echizen Fukui, ceremonial proxy visits were maintained generation after generation until the Meiji Restoration. ===Cultural properties=== === Tochigi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property === * Rōmon - Donated by the Mizuno family, lords of Yūki Castle; construction took 16 years from Hōreki 4 to Meiwa 7 ({{ill|lt=1770|Q7683|WD=Q7683|1=1770}}). Restoration was carried out from 2017 to 2018. === Oyama City designated intangible cultural property === * Daidai Kagura - dedicated at the autumn annual festival. === Chinowa crossing === The chinowa ring at Takahashi Shrine has a rare square shape, about 2 m per side, not circular. The reason is unknown. It is made with one-sided reed as core, wrapped in makomo and bound with cogon-grass ropeYomiuri Shimbun Tochigi edition, August 3, 2017, p.29.. === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === * Kōten Kōkyūjo / National Priests’ Association corrected edition [{{NDLDC|1442211}} ''Engishiki, upper volume'' p.319] (Ōokayama Shoten, 1929) - Modern Digital Library ===External links=== *[https://www.facebook.com/TAKAHASHIJINJA Engishiki Takahashi Shrine] (official site) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかはししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takahashi Shrine (Q11670742)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takahashi Shrine (Q11670742)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Takahashi Shrine (Q11670742)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:栃木県の神社]] [[Category:下総国の式内社]] [[Category:栃木県の旧県社]] [[Category:小山市の歴史]] [[Category:小山市の建築物]] [[category:日本の食文化|神たかはししんしや]] [[Category:高橋氏|社たかはししんしや]] {{DEFAULTSORT:QTakahashi Shrine (Q11670742)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QTakahashi Shrine (Q11670742)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Takahashi Shrine (Q11670742)}}[[Category:qq]]

Restoration was carried out from 2017 to 2018. === Oyama City designated intangible cultural property === * Daidai Kagura - dedicated at the autumn annual festival. === Chinowa crossing === The chinowa ring at Takahashi Shrine has a rare square shape, about 2 m per side, not circular. The reason is unknown. It is made with one-sided reed as core, wrapped in makomo and bound with cogon-grass ropeYomiuri Shimbun Tochigi edition, August 3, 2017, p.29.. === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === * Kōten Kōkyūjo / National Priests’ Association corrected edition [{{NDLDC|1442211}} ''Engishiki, upper volume'' p.319] (Ōokayama Shoten, 1929) - Modern Digital Library ===External links=== *[https://www.facebook.com/TAKAHASHIJINJA Engishiki Takahashi Shrine] (official site) {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takahashi Shrine (Q11670742)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takahashi Shrine (Q11670742)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Takahashi Shrine (Q11670742)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:栃木県の神社]] [[Category:下総国の式内社]] [[Category:栃木県の旧県社]] [[Category:小山市の歴史]] [[Category:小山市の建築物]] [[category:日本の食文化|神たかはししんしや]] [[Category:高橋氏|社たかはししんしや]] [[Category:qq]]

[[JA:高椅神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11670742}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2014年7月}} {{Otheruses||{{ill|lt=Takahashi Shrine (Mishima)|Q11670742|WD=Q11670742|1=Takahashi Shrine (Q11670742)}} in {{ill|lt=Mishima|Q653478|WD=Q653478|1=Mishima, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}}} {{神社 | 名称=Takahashi Shrine | 画像=[[File:高椅神社楼門.jpg|270px|Takahashi Shrine rōmon]]
Rōmon (Tochigi Prefecture designated cultural property) | 所在地={{ill|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|Q44843|WD=Q44843|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Oyama|Q374511|WD=Q374511|1=Oyama, Tochigi}} Takahashi 702 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Iwaka Mutsukari no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q478504|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}}
Amakagami-no-Mikoto
Amayorozu-no-Mikoto | 別名=Carp Myōjin, Takahashi Myōjin | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）・former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} | 創建=(trad.) {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} 41 ({{ill|lt=111|Q30640|WD=Q30640|1=AD 111}}) | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | 例祭=First Saturday of October | 神事=Dedication of daidai-kagura | 地図={{Location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Takahashi Shrine|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250}} }} '''Takahashi Shrine''' (たかはしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Oyama|Q374511|WD=Q374511|1=Oyama, Tochigi}}, {{ill|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|Q44843|WD=Q44843|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was prefectural shrine. It is the oldest shrine whose main deity is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Iwaka Mutsukari no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, revered as the "ancestral deity of {{ill|lt=cuisine|Q1778821|WD=Q1778821|1=Cuisine}}." Associated deities are Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto, Amakagami-no-Mikoto, Amayorozu-no-Mikoto, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|1=Futsunushi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takaokami-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Homusubi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyouke-hime-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}. It has four branch shrines in {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}} and is worshiped by people in culinary professions. * Hida Takahashi Shrine ({{ill|lt=Gero Onsen Gassho Village|Q11360703|WD=Q11360703|1=Gero Onsen Gasshō-mura}}) * Keihoku Takahashi Shrine ({{ill|lt=Yoake no Mori|Q17209177|WD=Q17209177|1=Yoake no Mori}} in Nakatsugawa) * Kane Takahashi Shrine (inside Kane Shrine in Gifu) * Hida Takayama Takahashi Shrine (inside Hie Shrine in Takayama) ===History=== According to shrine tradition, in {{ill|lt=111|Q30640|WD=Q30640|1=AD 111}} (Keikō 41), when {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|1=Yamato Takeru}} campaigned eastward, he invited Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto, Amakagami-no-Mikoto, and Amayorozu-no-Mikoto here and prayed for victory. When Emperor Keikō later inspected the campaign sites, Iwaka Mutsukari no Mikoto, of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kashiwade no Omi|1=UNKNOWN}} line, was old and with imperial permission remained here. In {{ill|lt=684|Q31128|WD=Q31128|1=684}} ({{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}} 12), descendants of Iwaka Mutsukari who ruled this area were granted the surname Takahashi Ason, and in that year they merged their ancestral deity Iwaka Mutsukari at this shrine and called it Takahashi Shrine. In {{ill|lt=1029|Q19274|WD=Q19274|1=1029}} ({{ill|lt=Chōgen|Q1193557|WD=Q1193557|1=Chōgen}} 2), when a well was dug in the precincts, a large {{ill|lt=carp|Q81110|WD=Q81110|1=Common carp}} appeared. Reported to the capital, {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Ichijō|Q374087|WD=Q374087|1=Emperor Go-Ichijō}} deemed it truly miraculous and granted the imperial plaque "Japan’s sole carp-forbidden shrine." Since then, parishioners do not eat carp, do not use vessels painted with carp, and do not raise {{ill|lt=koinobori|Q779745|WD=Q779745|1=Koinobori}} in May. From this comes the alternate name '''Carp Myōjin'''. In the medieval period it was protected by the {{ill|lt=Yūki clan|Q2975399|WD=Q2975399|1=Yūki clan}} of {{ill|lt=Shimōsa Province|Q1053695|WD=Q1053695|1=Shimōsa Province}}. More than 20 documents remain, including donation letters, prayers, and practice records from castle lords {{ill|lt=Yūki Masaasahi|Q11606587|WD=Q11606587|1=Yūki Masaasahi}}, {{ill|lt=Yūki Masanao|Q11606589|WD=Q11606589|1=Yūki Masanao}}, {{ill|lt=Yūki Masakatsu|Q8062739|WD=Q8062739|1=Yūki Masakatsu}}, {{ill|lt=Yūki Harutomo|Q8062736|WD=Q8062736|1=Yūki Harutomo}}, and {{ill|lt=Yūki Hideyasu|Q1130929|WD=Q1130929|1=Yūki Hideyasu}}. Even after Hideyasu was transferred to Echizen Fukui, ceremonial proxy visits were maintained generation after generation until the Meiji Restoration. ===Cultural properties=== === Tochigi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property === * Rōmon - Donated by the Mizuno family, lords of Yūki Castle; construction took 16 years from Hōreki 4 to Meiwa 7 ({{ill|lt=1770|Q7683|WD=Q7683|1=1770}}). Restoration was carried out from 2017 to 2018. === Oyama City designated intangible cultural property === * Daidai Kagura - dedicated at the autumn annual festival. === Chinowa crossing === The chinowa ring at Takahashi Shrine has a rare square shape, about 2 m per side, not circular. The reason is unknown. It is made with one-sided reed as core, wrapped in makomo and bound with cogon-grass ropeYomiuri Shimbun Tochigi edition, August 3, 2017, p.29.. === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === * Kōten Kōkyūjo / National Priests’ Association corrected edition [{{NDLDC|1442211}} ''Engishiki, upper volume'' p.319] (Ōokayama Shoten, 1929) - Modern Digital Library ===External links=== *[https://www.facebook.com/TAKAHASHIJINJA Engishiki Takahashi Shrine] (official site) {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takahashi Shrine (Q11670742)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takahashi Shrine (Q11670742)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Takahashi Shrine (Q11670742)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:栃木県の神社]] [[Category:下総国の式内社]] [[Category:栃木県の旧県社]] [[Category:小山市の歴史]] [[Category:小山市の建築物]] [[category:日本の食文化|神たかはししんしや]] [[Category:高橋氏|社たかはししんしや]]

Amayorozu-no-Mikoto | 別名=Carp Myōjin, Takahashi Myōjin | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）・former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} | 創建=(trad.) {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} 41 ({{ill|lt=111|Q30640|WD=Q30640|1=AD 111}}) | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | 例祭=First Saturday of October | 神事=Dedication of daidai-kagura | 地図={{Location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Takahashi Shrine|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250}} }} '''Takahashi Shrine''' (たかはしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Oyama|Q374511|WD=Q374511|1=Oyama, Tochigi}}, {{ill|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|Q44843|WD=Q44843|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was prefectural shrine. It is the oldest shrine whose main deity is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Iwaka Mutsukari no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, revered as the "ancestral deity of {{ill|lt=cuisine|Q1778821|WD=Q1778821|1=Cuisine}}." Associated deities are Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto, Amakagami-no-Mikoto, Amayorozu-no-Mikoto, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|1=Futsunushi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takaokami-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Homusubi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyouke-hime-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}. It has four branch shrines in {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}} and is worshiped by people in culinary professions. * Hida Takahashi Shrine ({{ill|lt=Gero Onsen Gassho Village|Q11360703|WD=Q11360703|1=Gero Onsen Gasshō-mura}}) * Keihoku Takahashi Shrine ({{ill|lt=Yoake no Mori|Q17209177|WD=Q17209177|1=Yoake no Mori}} in Nakatsugawa) * Kane Takahashi Shrine (inside Kane Shrine in Gifu) * Hida Takayama Takahashi Shrine (inside Hie Shrine in Takayama) ===History=== According to shrine tradition, in {{ill|lt=111|Q30640|WD=Q30640|1=AD 111}} (Keikō 41), when {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|1=Yamato Takeru}} campaigned eastward, he invited Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto, Amakagami-no-Mikoto, and Amayorozu-no-Mikoto here and prayed for victory. When Emperor Keikō later inspected the campaign sites, Iwaka Mutsukari no Mikoto, of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kashiwade no Omi|1=UNKNOWN}} line, was old and with imperial permission remained here. In {{ill|lt=684|Q31128|WD=Q31128|1=684}} ({{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}} 12), descendants of Iwaka Mutsukari who ruled this area were granted the surname Takahashi Ason, and in that year they merged their ancestral deity Iwaka Mutsukari at this shrine and called it Takahashi Shrine. In {{ill|lt=1029|Q19274|WD=Q19274|1=1029}} ({{ill|lt=Chōgen|Q1193557|WD=Q1193557|1=Chōgen}} 2), when a well was dug in the precincts, a large {{ill|lt=carp|Q81110|WD=Q81110|1=Common carp}} appeared. Reported to the capital, {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Ichijō|Q374087|WD=Q374087|1=Emperor Go-Ichijō}} deemed it truly miraculous and granted the imperial plaque "Japan’s sole carp-forbidden shrine." Since then, parishioners do not eat carp, do not use vessels painted with carp, and do not raise {{ill|lt=koinobori|Q779745|WD=Q779745|1=Koinobori}} in May. From this comes the alternate name '''Carp Myōjin'''. In the medieval period it was protected by the {{ill|lt=Yūki clan|Q2975399|WD=Q2975399|1=Yūki clan}} of {{ill|lt=Shimōsa Province|Q1053695|WD=Q1053695|1=Shimōsa Province}}. More than 20 documents remain, including donation letters, prayers, and practice records from castle lords {{ill|lt=Yūki Masaasahi|Q11606587|WD=Q11606587|1=Yūki Masaasahi}}, {{ill|lt=Yūki Masanao|Q11606589|WD=Q11606589|1=Yūki Masanao}}, {{ill|lt=Yūki Masakatsu|Q8062739|WD=Q8062739|1=Yūki Masakatsu}}, {{ill|lt=Yūki Harutomo|Q8062736|WD=Q8062736|1=Yūki Harutomo}}, and {{ill|lt=Yūki Hideyasu|Q1130929|WD=Q1130929|1=Yūki Hideyasu}}. Even after Hideyasu was transferred to Echizen Fukui, ceremonial proxy visits were maintained generation after generation until the Meiji Restoration. ===Cultural properties=== === Tochigi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property === * Rōmon - Donated by the Mizuno family, lords of Yūki Castle; construction took 16 years from Hōreki 4 to Meiwa 7 ({{ill|lt=1770|Q7683|WD=Q7683|1=1770}}). Restoration was carried out from 2017 to 2018. === Oyama City designated intangible cultural property === * Daidai Kagura - dedicated at the autumn annual festival. === Chinowa crossing === The chinowa ring at Takahashi Shrine has a rare square shape, about 2 m per side, not circular. The reason is unknown. It is made with one-sided reed as core, wrapped in makomo and bound with cogon-grass ropeYomiuri Shimbun Tochigi edition, August 3, 2017, p.29.. === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === * Kōten Kōkyūjo / National Priests’ Association corrected edition [{{NDLDC|1442211}} ''Engishiki, upper volume'' p.319] (Ōokayama Shoten, 1929) - Modern Digital Library ===External links=== *[https://www.facebook.com/TAKAHASHIJINJA Engishiki Takahashi Shrine] (official site) {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takahashi Shrine (Q11670742)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takahashi Shrine (Q11670742)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Takahashi Shrine (Q11670742)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:栃木県の神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:栃木県の旧県社]] [[Category:小山市の歴史]] [[Category:小山市の建築物]] [[category:日本の食文化|神たかはししんしや]] [[Category:高橋氏|社たかはししんしや]]

[[JA:高椅神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11670742}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2014年7月}} {{Otheruses||{{ill|lt=Takahashi Shrine (Mishima)|Q11670742|WD=Q11670742|1=Takahashi Shrine (Q11670742)}} in {{ill|lt=Mishima|Q653478|WD=Q653478|1=Mishima, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}}} {{神社 | 名称=Takahashi Shrine | 画像=[[File:高椅神社楼門.jpg|270px|Takahashi Shrine rōmon]]
Rōmon (Tochigi Prefecture designated cultural property) | 所在地={{ill|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|Q44843|WD=Q44843|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Oyama|Q374511|WD=Q374511|1=Oyama, Tochigi}} Takahashi 702 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Iwaka Mutsukari no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q478504|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}}
Amakagami-no-Mikoto
Amayorozu-no-Mikoto | 別名=Carp Myōjin, Takahashi Myōjin | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）・former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} | 創建=(trad.) {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} 41 ({{ill|lt=111|Q30640|WD=Q30640|1=AD 111}}) | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | 例祭=First Saturday of October | 神事=Dedication of daidai-kagura | 地図={{Location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Takahashi Shrine|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250}} }} '''Takahashi Shrine''' (たかはしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Oyama|Q374511|WD=Q374511|1=Oyama, Tochigi}}, {{ill|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|Q44843|WD=Q44843|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was prefectural shrine. It is the oldest shrine whose main deity is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Iwaka Mutsukari no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, revered as the "ancestral deity of {{ill|lt=cuisine|Q1778821|WD=Q1778821|1=Cuisine}}." Associated deities are Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto, Amakagami-no-Mikoto, Amayorozu-no-Mikoto, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|1=Futsunushi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takaokami-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Homusubi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyouke-hime-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}. It has four branch shrines in {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}} and is worshiped by people in culinary professions. * Hida Takahashi Shrine ({{ill|lt=Gero Onsen Gassho Village|Q11360703|WD=Q11360703|1=Gero Onsen Gasshō-mura}}) * Keihoku Takahashi Shrine ({{ill|lt=Yoake no Mori|Q17209177|WD=Q17209177|1=Yoake no Mori}} in Nakatsugawa) * Kane Takahashi Shrine (inside Kane Shrine in Gifu) * Hida Takayama Takahashi Shrine (inside Hie Shrine in Takayama) ===History=== According to shrine tradition, in {{ill|lt=111|Q30640|WD=Q30640|1=AD 111}} (Keikō 41), when {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|1=Yamato Takeru}} campaigned eastward, he invited Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto, Amakagami-no-Mikoto, and Amayorozu-no-Mikoto here and prayed for victory. When Emperor Keikō later inspected the campaign sites, Iwaka Mutsukari no Mikoto, of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kashiwade no Omi|1=UNKNOWN}} line, was old and with imperial permission remained here. In {{ill|lt=684|Q31128|WD=Q31128|1=684}} ({{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}} 12), descendants of Iwaka Mutsukari who ruled this area were granted the surname Takahashi Ason, and in that year they merged their ancestral deity Iwaka Mutsukari at this shrine and called it Takahashi Shrine. In {{ill|lt=1029|Q19274|WD=Q19274|1=1029}} ({{ill|lt=Chōgen|Q1193557|WD=Q1193557|1=Chōgen}} 2), when a well was dug in the precincts, a large {{ill|lt=carp|Q81110|WD=Q81110|1=Common carp}} appeared. Reported to the capital, {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Ichijō|Q374087|WD=Q374087|1=Emperor Go-Ichijō}} deemed it truly miraculous and granted the imperial plaque "Japan’s sole carp-forbidden shrine." Since then, parishioners do not eat carp, do not use vessels painted with carp, and do not raise {{ill|lt=koinobori|Q779745|WD=Q779745|1=Koinobori}} in May. From this comes the alternate name '''Carp Myōjin'''. In the medieval period it was protected by the {{ill|lt=Yūki clan|Q2975399|WD=Q2975399|1=Yūki clan}} of {{ill|lt=Shimōsa Province|Q1053695|WD=Q1053695|1=Shimōsa Province}}. More than 20 documents remain, including donation letters, prayers, and practice records from castle lords {{ill|lt=Yūki Masaasahi|Q11606587|WD=Q11606587|1=Yūki Masaasahi}}, {{ill|lt=Yūki Masanao|Q11606589|WD=Q11606589|1=Yūki Masanao}}, {{ill|lt=Yūki Masakatsu|Q8062739|WD=Q8062739|1=Yūki Masakatsu}}, {{ill|lt=Yūki Harutomo|Q8062736|WD=Q8062736|1=Yūki Harutomo}}, and {{ill|lt=Yūki Hideyasu|Q1130929|WD=Q1130929|1=Yūki Hideyasu}}. Even after Hideyasu was transferred to Echizen Fukui, ceremonial proxy visits were maintained generation after generation until the Meiji Restoration. ===Cultural properties=== === Tochigi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property === * Rōmon - Donated by the Mizuno family, lords of Yūki Castle; construction took 16 years from Hōreki 4 to Meiwa 7 ({{ill|lt=1770|Q7683|WD=Q7683|1=1770}}). Restoration was carried out from 2017 to 2018. === Oyama City designated intangible cultural property === * Daidai Kagura - dedicated at the autumn annual festival. === Chinowa crossing === The chinowa ring at Takahashi Shrine has a rare square shape, about 2 m per side, not circular. The reason is unknown. It is made with one-sided reed as core, wrapped in makomo and bound with cogon-grass ropeYomiuri Shimbun Tochigi edition, August 3, 2017, p.29.. === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === * Kōten Kōkyūjo / National Priests’ Association corrected edition [{{NDLDC|1442211}} ''Engishiki, upper volume'' p.319] (Ōokayama Shoten, 1929) - Modern Digital Library ===External links=== *[https://www.facebook.com/TAKAHASHIJINJA Engishiki Takahashi Shrine] (official site) {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takahashi Shrine (Q11670742)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takahashi Shrine (Q11670742)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Takahashi Shrine (Q11670742)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:栃木県の神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:栃木県の旧県社]] [[Category:History of Koyama]] [[Category:小山市の建築物]] [[Category:Japanese cuisine]] [[Category:高橋氏|社たかはししんしや]]

From this comes the alternate name '''Carp Myōjin'''. In the medieval period it was protected by the {{ill|lt=Yūki clan|Q2975399|WD=Q2975399|1=Yūki clan}} of {{ill|lt=Shimōsa Province|Q1053695|WD=Q1053695|1=Shimōsa Province}}. More than 20 documents remain, including donation letters, prayers, and practice records from castle lords {{ill|lt=Yūki Masaasahi|Q11606587|WD=Q11606587|1=Yūki Masaasahi}}, {{ill|lt=Yūki Masanao|Q11606589|WD=Q11606589|1=Yūki Masanao}}, {{ill|lt=Yūki Masakatsu|Q8062739|WD=Q8062739|1=Yūki Masakatsu}}, {{ill|lt=Yūki Harutomo|Q8062736|WD=Q8062736|1=Yūki Harutomo}}, and {{ill|lt=Yūki Hideyasu|Q1130929|WD=Q1130929|1=Yūki Hideyasu}}. Even after Hideyasu was transferred to Echizen Fukui, ceremonial proxy visits were maintained generation after generation until the Meiji Restoration. ===Cultural properties=== === Tochigi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property === * Rōmon - Donated by the Mizuno family, lords of Yūki Castle; construction took 16 years from Hōreki 4 to Meiwa 7 ({{ill|lt=1770|Q7683|WD=Q7683|1=1770}}). Restoration was carried out from 2017 to 2018. === Oyama City designated intangible cultural property === * Daidai Kagura - dedicated at the autumn annual festival. === Chinowa crossing === The chinowa ring at Takahashi Shrine has a rare square shape, about 2 m per side, not circular. The reason is unknown. It is made with one-sided reed as core, wrapped in makomo and bound with cogon-grass ropeYomiuri Shimbun Tochigi edition, August 3, 2017, p.29.. === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === * Kōten Kōkyūjo / National Priests’ Association corrected edition [{{NDLDC|1442211}} ''Engishiki, upper volume'' p.319] (Ōokayama Shoten, 1929) - Modern Digital Library ===External links=== *[https://www.facebook.com/TAKAHASHIJINJA Engishiki Takahashi Shrine] (official site) {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takahashi Shrine (Q11670742)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takahashi Shrine (Q11670742)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Takahashi Shrine (Q11670742)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:栃木県の旧県社]] [[Category:History of Koyama]] [[Category:小山市の建築物]] [[Category:Japanese cuisine]] [[Category:高橋氏|社たかはししんしや]]
